summaries.90550: Root cause narrowed down in comments to nesting a MaterialApp inside another MaterialApp — removing the inner MaterialApp stops the rebuild. Verified on stable 2.5.0/2.5.1 and master; does not reproduce on 2.2.3. A comment links PR #143249 as potentially related via a TextFormField controller listener bug. No fix landed; issue remains open with users still asking for a workaround.
summaries.137667: Issue confirmed Android-only; does not reproduce on iOS. Reproduction is device-specific: confirmed on Realme 6 (Android 11) and Realme X2 Pro but not on Pixel 3A (Android 12), Galaxy A5/J7, or Pixel 7 Pro (Android 14/15). A commenter identified that on affected devices `InputConnectionAdaptor.closeConnection` is called twice (on lock and again on unlock) instead of once, and the IME sends extra `getTextBeforeCursor`/`getExtractedText` calls on an inactive InputConnection. A working workaround using `WidgetsBindingObserver` to unfocus/re-focus via the app lifecycle was provided. Investigation stalled after the test device lost USB connectivity.
summaries.140953: Issue confirmed Android-only; works correctly on iOS Flutter apps and native Android WebView. Root cause discussion points to Scaffold not correctly calculating keyboard inset height in time — one commenter observed the height calculation succeeds when a debug breakpoint delays keyboard ejection. A JS-based workaround using `flutter_keyboard_visibility` + `document.activeElement.scrollIntoView()` was shared. One user noted `flutter_screenutil` 5.9.0 with `useInheritedMediaQuery: true` resolved the issue for them (related to #126585). No official fix; issue remains open.
summaries.168886: Root cause identified in comments: `KeyEvent.getSource()` on Android returns a combined bitmask (e.g., `0x301 = SOURCE_KEYBOARD | SOURCE_DPAD`), but `_convertDeviceType` in `hardware_keyboard.dart` performs exact equality checks against individual source constants, so combined values always fall through to the `keyboard` default. Fix requires bitwise AND checks instead of equality. Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.33. No PR yet; currently open and awaiting a fix.
summaries.103086: Issue confirmed reproducible on iOS web (Safari and Chrome, which share the same engine on iOS) on stable 2.10.5 and master 2.13. Root cause noted as `DefaultTextEditingStrategy.handleChange` responding to a DOM event that resets cursor position when `readOnly=true`. A comment notes the behavior is inconsistent with native web `readonly` inputs. Assignee was eventually unassigned after no status updates. Issue is still open and stalled.
summaries.88910: Comments confirm the bug is a gesture conflict: `SelectableText` consumes mouse drag events across its entire bounding box, preventing `PageView` from receiving horizontal drag gestures for page-turning. Issue affects web and all desktop platforms; iOS native is unaffected. Custom `ScrollBehavior` with mouse drag support was tried but did not resolve the conflict — it only inverted the problem in Flutter 3.0 (page turns took over, making text selection impossible). Removing `SelectableText` in favor of `Text` restores page-turning. Stalled with no fix.
summaries.184607: The single comment requests a minimal reproducible code sample and asks whether custom fonts or specific languages/scripts are involved. A follow-up comment from the reporter says pasting 10,000–20,000+ words into a TextField causes the ANR; degradation starts around 20,000 words on both physical devices and simulators. A triage comment links this to existing issues #90063 and #96173 and asks whether it is a duplicate. Currently waiting on customer response.
summaries.90256: The reporter originally could not reproduce in 2.5.1 and the issue was closed, but was then reopened after the reporter reproduced it again in 2.5.1. Platform-specific investigation showed it reproduced 100% on iPad Pro (11-inch, 3rd gen) iOS 15.0 simulator but not on iPhone 13 Pro Max iOS 15.0 simulator. Assignee was eventually removed after long inactivity. No root cause identified and no fix landed; issue is stalled.
summaries.115829: Issue confirmed on iOS PWA only (not on iOS Safari non-PWA or Android). A commenter noted the same broken-tap-region behavior can be triggered by navigating to an external URL with `launchUrl` using `webOnlyWindowName: '_self'` — not just TextField focus — suggesting the root cause is broader than text input. No root cause identified and no fix landed; stalled.
summaries.90063: Comments identify two contributing performance bottlenecks: (1) the entire text content is serialized and sent over the platform channel on every change, and (2) text layout of large content is slow. The `TextEditingDelta` / `DeltaTextInputClient` API was introduced as a potential path to delta-based updates, and engine PR flutter/engine#29036 improved macOS. As of the latest comments, all desktop platforms support deltas via a custom `DeltaTextInputClient`, but `TextField` has not been migrated to use it, and `TextInput.setEditingState` still sends the full text. Issue affects mobile and web in addition to desktop. Currently stalled with no concrete fix timeline.
summaries.71144: Root cause identified as Android-version-specific: on Android 9+ devices (tested on Pixel 6 Android 13, Xiaomi Mi A2 Android 10, Samsung XCover6 Pro Android 13), Flutter's `FlutterView` does not initially have focus for hardware key dispatch, so volume and other hardware keys are not routed to the Flutter key event system until the soft keyboard is opened or a text key is pressed. Works correctly on Android ≤6 devices and is an emulator-specific non-issue. Multiple workarounds shared: invoking `TextInput.show`/`TextInput.hide`, using `WidgetsBinding` to autofocus a hidden TextField then immediately redirect focus, and a native Android workaround overriding `onKeyDown` in `MainActivity` or using `pview.requestFocus(); view.requestFocus()` in `onStart`.
summaries.105184: Root cause identified as `MediaQuery.of(context)` triggering a rebuild on every frame during keyboard animation, because MediaQuery emits a change notification per frame as the keyboard slides in/out. This was introduced as a regression relative to Flutter 2.8 — on iOS, rebuilds went from 2 times (2.8) to 20+ times (3.x). PR #97928 was cited as the intended fix, and PR #114459 (Callum's) was mentioned as a follow-up solution. Workarounds include using `MediaQuery.sizeOf(context)` instead of `MediaQuery.of(context)`, using a `Builder` widget to scope rebuilds, or using `LayoutBuilder`. Issue persists as of Flutter 3.22.2 with users reporting janks on complex screens.
